Package de.uka.ilkd.key.smt.newsmt2

Interface SMTHandler

All Known Implementing Classes:
BooleanConnectiveHandler, CastHandler, CastingFunctionsHandler, DefinedSymbolsHandler, FieldConstantHandler, FloatHandler, FloatRemainderHandler, InstanceOfHandler, IntegerOpHandler, LogicalVariableHandler, NumberConstantsHandler, PolymorphicHandler, QuantifierHandler, SeqDefHandler, SumProdHandler, UninterpretedSymbolsHandler, UpdateHandler
public interface SMTHandler
General interface for routines that translate particular KeY data structures to SMT. SMT handlers are loaded via a ServiceLoader. To implement a new handler, implement this interface and add the classname to a file that ServiceLoader reads for SMTHandler.

Procedure

SMT handlers are created using the default constructor without parameters They are always used within the same proof, but possibly for several proof obligations. After creation, the init(MasterHandler, Services, Properties, String[]) method is called that injects the Services object belonging to the proof. During translation, an SMT handler can be asked via canHandle(Term) if it can translate a term into smt. If it returns true, the method handle(MasterHandler, Term) will be called which returns the SMT result in form of an SExpr.

  • Nested Class Summary

    Nested Classes
    Modifier and Type
    Interface
    Description
    static enum 
    SMTHandler.Capability
    An enumeration of the possible answers of an handler to the canHandle(Term) method.

  • Method Details

    • init

      void init(MasterHandler masterHandler, Services services, Properties handlerSnippets, String[] handlerOptions) throws IOException
      Initialise this handler. This method is only called once after creation and before any other method is called. This method may also allocate additional resources that it needs for translation.
      Parameters:
      masterHandler - the MasterHandler coordinating the other handlers (including the one at hand)
      services - the non-null services object which is relevant for this handler
      handlerSnippets - the snippets loaded for this handler, null if no snippet property file is available for this handler
      handlerOptions - arbitrary options for the handler to take into account
      Throws:
      IOException - if resources cannot be read.

    • canHandle

      default SMTHandler.Capability canHandle(Term term)
      Query if this handler can translate a term. Test if this particular term can be translated. Usually this requires checking whether the toplevel operator of the term is in the list of supported operators, but the handler can also choose to use other aspects of the term to decide.
      Parameters:
      term - a non-null term to translate
      Returns:
      SMTHandler.Capability.YES_THIS_OPERATOR if this handler can successfully translate any term with the same toplevel operator, SMTHandler.Capability.YES_THIS_INSTANCE if this handler can successfully translate this particular term, SMTHandler.Capability.UNABLE if this handler cannot deal with the term.

    • canHandle

      boolean canHandle(Operator op)
      Query if this handler can translate an operator. Test if this handler can translate any term with the given argument top level operator.
      Parameters:
      op - a non-null operator to translate
      Returns:
      true if this handler can successfully translate all terms with op as toplevel operator

    • handle

      SExpr handle(MasterHandler trans, Term term) throws SMTTranslationException
      Translate the given term into an SMT SExpression.

      This method will only be called if canHandle(Term) returned true for the same term argument.

      The translation may add to the set of assumptions and declarations using corresponding calls to the MasterHandler that it receives.

      Parameters:
      trans - the non-null master handler to which it belongs
      term - the non-null term to translate
      Returns:
      an SExpr representing the term
      Throws:
      SMTTranslationException - if the translation fails unexpectedly.

    • getProperties

      default List<SMTHandlerProperty<?>> getProperties()
      Any handler can offer a collection of properties that can be set in the GUI/script to control the translation. The returned list is used in SMTHandlerServices and will not be modified.
      Returns:
      the non-null list of properties that this handler supports and reads.
